title: Xtra Juicy Chinese Pork Bao
description: A briny and juicy Chinese dish featuring caramel sauce and long-fermentation-dough, perfect for showing off, with raw-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Combine flour, yeast, salt, and sugar in a large mixing bowl.
Add chopped pork shoulder, scallions, soy sauce, sesame oil, and grated ginger to the bowl and mix well.
Knead the mixture for 10 minutes until a smooth dough forms.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment for 2 hours.
Preheat a steamer basket over boiling water.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk and place a tablespoon of caramel sauce in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling and seal the edges.
Place the buns in the steamer basket and steam for 15 minutes.
Serve the buns hot with additional caramel sauce for dipping.
